hypothesis: If a diffusion model framework combining an equivariant graph neural network backbone with an energy-based denoising variant that incorporates Hamiltonian Monte Carlo refinement is applied to amorphous material generation, then it will produce structurally valid amorphous configurations—as measured by radial distribution functions, bond angle distributions, structure factors, and potential energy distributions—that are comparable to those obtained from classical molecular dynamics simulations, because the energy-based score matching with HMC equilibration enforces physically consistent local geometry during the reverse diffusion process.
